vocabulary.pinyin: zuǒ bian nà jiàn hóng sè de
vocabulary.english: That red one on the left
The white one on the right
Give the big one to him
Give the small one to me

phrases.pinyin: zuǒ bian nà jiàn hóng sè de
phrases.english: That red one on the left
The white one on the right
Give the big one to him
Give the small one to me

key_sentences.chinese: 左边那件红色的是我的。
key_sentences.pinyin: Zuǒ bian nà jiàn hóng sè de shì wǒ de.
key_sentences.english: The red one on the left is mine.

key_sentences.chinese: 这件衣服是什么颜色的？
key_sentences.pinyin: Zhè jiàn yī fu shì shén me yán sè de?
key_sentences.english: What colour is this item of clothing?

key_sentences.pinyin: Nà jiàn bái sè de bǐ zhè jiàn dà.
key_sentences.english: That white one is bigger than this one.

key_sentences.pinyin: Qǐng cóng páng biān de mén jìn lái.
key_sentences.english: Please enter through the door next to it.

key_sentences.pinyin: Tā zhàn zài mén de yòu bian.
key_sentences.english: He is standing to the right of the door.

key_sentences.chinese: 黑色的那件是大号还是小号？
key_sentences.pinyin: Hēi sè de nà jiàn shì dà hào hái shì xiǎo hào?
key_sentences.english: Is that black item a large or small size?

grammar_points.title: 「的」字短语 — Nominal Clause with de
grammar_points.explanation: When 的 (de) follows an adjective or a modifier, it turns the phrase into a noun equivalent meaning 'the one that is…'. Structure: Adjective/Modifier + 的 = noun phrase. For example: 红色的 means 'the red one'; 我的 means 'mine'. This is used when the noun is clear from context and does not need to be repeated.

examples.chinese: 左边那件红色的是我的。
examples.pinyin: Zuǒ bian nà jiàn hóng sè de shì wǒ de.
examples.english: The red one on the left is mine.

examples.pinyin: Dà de gěi tā, xiǎo de gěi wǒ.
examples.english: Give him the big one; give me the small one.

exercises.question: What does 红色的 mean when used as a noun phrase?
Which sentence uses the '的' nominal phrase correctly?
exercises.correct_pinyin: Bái sè de shì wǒ de.

exercises.question: Fill in: 大___给他，小___给我。(Give him the big one; give me the small one.)
How do you say 'the white one on the right'?

grammar_points.title: 结构助词「着」— The Structural Particle zhe
grammar_points.explanation: 着 (zhe) is placed after a verb to describe an ongoing state or the manner in which an action is performed. Structure: Verb + 着. It is different from 在 + verb (which describes an action in progress). 着 often describes a continuous state: 门开着 (the door is open), 他站着 (he is standing).

examples.pinyin: Mén kāi zhe, qǐng jìn lái.
examples.english: The door is open; please come in.
He is standing waiting for me.

exercises.question: What does 着 (zhe) indicate after a verb?
Which sentence means 'The door is open'?
Fill in: 她坐___看书。(She is sitting reading a book.)
Which phrase means 'enter through the door'?

grammar_points.title: 名词谓语句 — Nominal Predicate Sentence
grammar_points.explanation: In Chinese, a noun or noun phrase can directly serve as the predicate of a sentence without a verb like 是 (shì). This is mainly used for time, age, price, or other measurable facts. Structure: Subject + Noun Phrase (as predicate). Example: 今天星期三。(Today is Wednesday.) Note: 是 is NOT used in these sentences.

examples.pinyin: Zhè jiàn yī fu yī bǎi kuài qián.
examples.english: This item of clothing is 100 yuan.

exercises.question: Which sentence is a correct nominal predicate sentence?
What is mainly expressed with nominal predicate sentences?
exercises.correct: Time, age, price, or measurable facts
options: Time, age, price, or measurable facts

exercises.question: Fill in: 他___三十岁。(He is thirty years old.) — Which word is NOT needed?

lines.speaker: A
lines.chinese: 请问，左边那件红色的是多少钱？
lines.pinyin: Qǐng wèn, zuǒ bian nà jiàn hóng sè de shì duō shǎo qián?
lines.english: Excuse me, how much is that red one on the left?

lines.speaker: B
lines.chinese: 左边那件红色的是一百块。
lines.pinyin: Zuǒ bian nà jiàn hóng sè de shì yī bǎi kuài.
lines.english: The red one on the left is 100 yuan.

lines.speaker: A
lines.chinese: 有白色的吗？我想要白色的。
lines.pinyin: Yǒu bái sè de ma? Wǒ xiǎng yào bái sè de.
lines.english: Do you have a white one? I'd like the white one.

lines.speaker: B
lines.chinese: 有，白色的在右边，旁边还有黑色的。
lines.pinyin: Yǒu, bái sè de zài yòu bian, páng biān hái yǒu hēi sè de.
lines.english: Yes, the white one is on the right; there's also a black one next to it.

lines.speaker: A
lines.chinese: 我要大号的，请问从哪个门进去？
lines.pinyin: Wǒ yào dà hào de, qǐng wèn cóng nǎ gè mén jìn qù?
lines.english: I want the large size. Which door do I enter from?

lines.speaker: B
lines.chinese: 从旁边那个门进去，里面有大小两种。
lines.pinyin: Cóng páng biān nà gè mén jìn qù, lǐ miàn yǒu dà xiǎo liǎng zhǒng.
lines.english: Enter through the door next to it; inside there are two sizes.

lines.speaker: A
lines.chinese: 我的房间门开着，你进来吧。
lines.pinyin: Wǒ de fáng jiān mén kāi zhe, nǐ jìn lái ba.
lines.english: My room door is open; come on in.

lines.speaker: B
lines.chinese: 好的，你的书在左边还是右边？
lines.pinyin: Hǎo de, nǐ de shū zài zuǒ bian hái shì yòu bian?
lines.english: Okay, is your book on the left or the right?

lines.speaker: A
lines.chinese: 在桌子的右边，旁边有一件黑色的衣服。
lines.pinyin: Zài zhuō zi de yòu bian, páng biān yǒu yī jiàn hēi sè de yī fu.
lines.english: On the right side of the desk; there's a black piece of clothing next to it.

lines.speaker: B
lines.pinyin: Dà de hái shì xiǎo de?
lines.english: The big one or the small one?

lines.speaker: A
lines.chinese: 大的，是我新买的红色衣服旁边那件。
lines.pinyin: Dà de, shì wǒ xīn mǎi de hóng sè yī fu páng biān nà jiàn.
lines.english: The big one — it's the one next to the red clothes I just bought.
